 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","title":"Instrument end-to-end resize timeline with stage-level latency and queue metrics","description":"Add high-resolution timing probes covering input intent, scheduler queueing, logical reflow, render prep, and presentation. Emit structured events suitable for flamegraphs and per-stage attribution.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoMountain","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:43.805837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:22:35.656542Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:22:27.169768Z","close_reason":"Implemented resize-stage timeline instrumentation and tests/docs; global fmt/clippy blockers remain pre-existing outside this scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reflow","resize","telemetry","trace","track-baseline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:43.805837Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.481309Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":68,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","author":"IndigoMountain","text":"Implemented stage-level resize timeline instrumentation in the simulation baseline pipeline.

Changed files:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/simulation.rs
  - Added resize timeline stage model (`input_intent`, `scheduler_queueing`, `logical_reflow`, `render_prep`, `presentation`)
  - Added queue-depth attribution on scheduler stage (`depth_before`, `depth_after`)
  - Added structured timeline artifacts (`ResizeTimeline*`) with `stage_summary()` and `flame_samples()`
  - Added execution APIs: `execute_until_with_resize_timeline` and `execute_all_with_resize_timeline`
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/simulation_resize_suite.rs
  - Added fixture-level timeline coverage/ordering/queue-metric test
- docs/resize-baseline-scenarios.md
  - Documented timeline instrumentation contract and APIs

Validation:
- rustfmt +nightly --edition 2024 on touched files: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib execute_all_with_resize_timeline_records_stage_probes -- --nocapture: PASS
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test simulation_resize_suite resize_suite_timeline_probes_cover_required_stages -- --nocapture: PASS
- cargo check --all-targets: PASS (with many pre-existing warnings)
- cargo fmt --check: FAIL due pre-existing formatting drift in unrelated files
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: FAIL due extensive pre-existing unrelated lint violations across workspace

Notes:
- Intentionally avoided runtime.rs/main.rs overlap due active file reservations by other agents.
- This provides structured per-stage attribution artifacts for baseline SLO/dossier follow-on beads.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:22:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","title":"Profile lock contention and memory behavior in terminal/mux/render resize paths","description":"Produce lock-hold and contention profiles plus memory growth curves under storm scenarios. Identify pathological lock convoys and allocation hotspots with concrete evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","notes":"WhiteCave 2026-02-14 verification: target/debug/ft simulate run fixtures/simulations/resize_baseline/resize_single_pane_scrollback.yaml --json still fails with unknown action generate_scrollback (FT-9001). Compile-harness blocker appears cleared: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-whitecave-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test simulation_resize_suite --no-run succeeds and emits test executable. Updated docs/resize-lock-memory-profile-wa-1u90p.1.3.md accordingly.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletDune","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:43.910134Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T02:22:41.071606Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["locks","memory","profiling","reflow","resize","track-baseline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:43.910134Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.586836Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":58,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"DustySnow","text":"Implemented draft profiling patch in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs: storage lock wait/hold telemetry (avg/max + contention events), cursor snapshot memory sampling + thresholds, and health-snapshot warnings for lock contention/memory growth. Added unit tests for new RuntimeMetrics APIs. Validation attempts: cargo fmt --check fails due broad existing workspace formatting drift; cargo check --all-targets and cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings fail due extensive pre-existing workspace issues unrelated to this bead. Coordinating with StormyIsland due shared runtime.rs reservation overlap.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:48Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"MagentaValley follow-up: verified runtime profiling instrumentation is present in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs (storage lock wait/hold metrics, contention counters, cursor snapshot memory samples, warning thresholds, and lock profiling helper). Attempted focused validation with isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR; currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated compile failures in other modules (e.g., event_id.rs missing imports, recorder_* test imports, simulation.rs usize::from(u32) errors). No new runtime.rs-specific compile/test regressions surfaced in this pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:13:10Z"},{"id":74,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"MagentaValley addendum: added runtime warning-threshold unit tests in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs for new telemetry surfaces: storage_lock_contention_warning_threshold_fires, storage_lock_hold_warning_threshold_fires, cursor_snapshot_memory_warning_threshold_fires. Baseline validation now improved: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-magenta cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes. Note: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing lib-test compile issues in recorder/event_id modules, so runtime lib-test execution is still branch-blocked outside this scope.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:26:01Z"},{"id":153,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"BronzeMill","text":"2026-02-14 BronzeMill: taking compile-unblock slice for this bead. Reproduced current blockers with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-bronzemill cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets. Errors: runtime.rs E0609 (RuntimeHandle missing wezterm_handle), search/chunk_vector_store.rs E0277 (rusqlite FromSql<u64>), resize_scheduler.rs E0063 (test ResizeSchedulerConfig initializers missing new fields). Reserved runtime.rs + chunk_vector_store.rs and coordinating resize_scheduler.rs conflict with TopazGorge before touching that file.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:16:15Z"},{"id":154,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"VioletDune","text":"VioletDune claiming compile-unblock slice on wa-1u90p.1.3. Starting by reproducing current compile blockers and fixing high-signal failures reported in prior note (workflows Instant mismatches, resize_scheduler const-fn issue, runtime handle mismatch, and any vector/chunk store typing errors). Will post touched file list + check output summary after first pass.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:16:32Z"},{"id":156,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"BronzeMill","text":"2026-02-14 BronzeMill: completed compile-unblock sub-slice on two files. Fixes landed: (1) crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs E0609 by adding/wiring RuntimeHandle.wezterm_handle for health probe warnings; (2) crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs E0277 by reading DB offsets as i64 and performing checked u64 conversions with IntegralValueOutOfRange mapping. Validation: rustfmt --edition 2024 on touched files ✅; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-bronzemill cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets now no longer reports runtime/chunk_vector_store errors. Remaining blockers from this check: resize_scheduler.rs E0063 at lines 1371/1406 (missing new ResizeSchedulerConfig fields in tests), plus unrelated existing test compile error in tests/proptest_dataflow.rs (format string in prop_assert). Did not edit resize_scheduler due active reservation/ownership by TopazGorge; shared exact error coordinates via Agent Mail.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:20:13Z"},{"id":161,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"BronzeMill","text":"2026-02-14 BronzeMill follow-up: added two additional compile-unblock fixes after deeper all-target check surfaced new test blockers. (1) crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_dataflow.rs: removed failing  direct macro pattern by binding to  first, and replaced no-op edge_count test with deterministic equality assertion against snapshot edge count. (2) crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_unix_socket.rs: restored required tokio extension trait imports (, ) so read_exact/write_all resolve. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-bronzemill cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test asupersync_unix_socket --no-run ✅; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-bronzemill cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets ✅ (warnings only). Remaining failures are non-check gates: cargo fmt --check reports broad workspace drift in many unrelated files; clippy -D warnings fails on existing repo lint debt (including resize_scheduler/chunking and style lints) beyond this compile-unblock slice.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:26:16Z"},{"id":162,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"BronzeMill","text":"2026-02-14 BronzeMill correction/update:

Additional compile-unblock fixes landed after deeper all-target checks.

1) `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_dataflow.rs`
- replaced direct `prop_assert!(matches!(... CycleDetected { .. }))` pattern with an intermediate boolean assertion (`cycle_detected`) to avoid macro format-string parse failures.
- replaced no-op edge_count check with deterministic assertion: `edge_count == snapshot().edges.len()`.

2) `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_unix_socket.rs`
- restored required extension trait imports: `tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt}` so `read_exact`/`write_all` resolve on `UnixStream`.

Validation:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-bronzemill cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test asupersync_unix_socket --no-run` ✅
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-bronzemill cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets` ✅ (warnings only)

Remaining non-check gate failures:
- `cargo fmt --check` still reports broad workspace drift in many unrelated files.
- `cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` still fails on existing lint debt in unrelated areas (including `resize_scheduler` and `chunking`) plus style lints.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:26:31Z"},{"id":169,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"VioletDune","text":"2026-02-14 VioletDune compile+baseline unblock slice: fixed workspace build blocker in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs simulate-run path by converting Err(frankenterm_core::Error::Runtime(...)) to anyhow via .into(). Validation: (1) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetdune cargo check --all-targets ✅ (warnings only); (2) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetdune cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets ✅; (3) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetdune cargo test -p frankenterm-core warning_threshold_fires --lib ✅ (3/3 runtime lock/memory warning threshold tests pass); (4) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetdune cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test simulation_resize_suite -- --nocapture ✅ (4/4); (5) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-violetdune cargo run -p frankenterm -- simulate run fixtures/simulations/resize_baseline/resize_single_pane_scrollback.yaml --json ✅ now parses/executes generate_scrollback and all expectations pass. Note: stale target/debug/ft binary from 2026-02-11 was the source of prior unknown-variant behavior.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:37:43Z"},{"id":170,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"VioletDune","text":"2026-02-14 VioletDune follow-up evidence: captured timeline artifacts for all four canonical resize-baseline fixtures via cargo-run simulate with --resize-timeline-json. New artifacts under evidence/wa-1u90p.1.3/summaries/: resize_single_pane_timeline.json, resize_multi_tab_storm_timeline.json, font_churn_multi_pane_timeline.json, mixed_scale_soak_timeline.json. Quick rollup (scenario -> executed_resize_events / logical_reflow.max_duration_ns / flame_samples): resize_single_pane_scrollback -> 8 / 3766917 / 40; resize_multi_tab_storm -> 24 / 325917 / 120; font_churn_multi_pane -> 24 / 216084 / 120; mixed_scale_soak -> 28 / 671167 / 140. Updated docs/resize-lock-memory-profile-wa-1u90p.1.3.md with this summary and revised next steps for wa-1u90p.1.5 handoff.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:40:57Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","author":"VioletDune","text":"2026-02-14 VioletDune continuation: added consolidated timeline rollup artifact evidence/wa-1u90p.1.3/summaries/resize_baseline_timeline_rollup_2026-02-14.json (4 canonical scenarios) with per-stage avg/p95/max and scheduler queue-depth peaks. Updated docs/resize-lock-memory-profile-wa-1u90p.1.3.md with explicit hotspot findings: (a) logical_reflow is dominant latency lane in all scenarios, (b) single-pane large-scrollback has largest isolated outlier (3,766,917 ns), (c) mixed_scale_soak has heaviest sustained tail pressure (logical_reflow p95 530,000 ns, highest multi-pane presentation tail), and (d) queue depth drains to zero in all scenarios (no persistent simulation-path backlog).","created_at":"2026-02-14T02:22:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","title":"Define hard SLOs: frame-time, interaction latency, artifact tolerance, and crash budget","description":"## Context
All later implementation and rollout decisions depend on explicit, measurable targets that reflect user perception and operational reliability.

## Scope
Define the authoritative SLO/SLA-style gate set for resize/reflow work, including frame pacing, interaction latency, artifact classes, and crash budgets.

## Deliverables
- SLO specification with p50/p95/p99 thresholds per workload class
- Hardware-tier matrix (low/mid/high) with expected behavior envelopes
- Pass/fail gate definitions for CI, soak, and go/no-go review
- Exception/degradation policy with trigger conditions and recovery criteria

## Why this matters
Without hard targets, optimization becomes subjective and release risk increases.

## Acceptance Criteria
- SLO document defines explicit numeric thresholds for frame-time, interaction latency, artifact incidence, and crash-free operation.
- Thresholds are mapped to specific benchmark/e2e harnesses and artifact outputs.
- Low/mid/high hardware tier expectations are explicit and testable.
- Degradation/rollback triggers are documented and referenced by rollout beads.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticBear","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.013858Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:50:07.000950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T00:50:07.000901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reflow","resize","slo","track-baseline","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.013858Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.693879Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:28.798467Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":117,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Prework completed while dependency-blocked: authored/refined docs/resize-performance-slos.md with explicit numeric thresholds plus exact metric-field mapping to implemented code surfaces (simulation ResizeTimeline API + runtime lock/memory telemetry). Added gate artifact contract and clear statement of current extraction limitation (no direct CLI ResizeTimeline JSON yet). Remaining work to close wa-1u90p.1.4 is final calibration/tightening after wa-1u90p.1.3 lock/memory profiling outputs and wa-1u90p.1.5 baseline dossier rollups land.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:28:41Z"},{"id":118,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Prework advancement: baseline artifact extraction gap partially closed by new CLI mode in ft simulate run. Added --resize-timeline-json (with --json) to emit full timeline artifact envelope (timeline + stage_summary + flame_samples + scenario reproducibility metadata). This reduces manual harness coupling for SLO evidence collection and should simplify final threshold calibration once wa-1u90p.1.3 profiles are finalized.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:41:08Z"},{"id":148,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","author":"RusticBear","text":"Updated SLO contract document to convert this bead from draft calibration notes into a frozen candidate contract with explicit dependency-bound refresh semantics.\n\nUpdated file:\n- docs/resize-performance-slos.md\n\nWhat changed:\n- Status promoted to Candidate v1.0 (numeric SLO contract frozen for CI/nightly/go-no-go gating)\n- Added explicit calibration intake section with  artifact pointers\n- Added current replay/build blocker taxonomy for fresh percentile extraction (E0308/E0277/E0609 + generate_scrollback replay mismatch)\n- Recorded that prior scheduler const-trait blocker (E0658) is now resolved by wa-1u90p.2.3\n- Tightened 'Current Gaps' and 'Dependency-Bound Sections' to distinguish enforceable thresholds now vs refresh-calibration after replay unblock\n\nAcceptance check vs bead scope:\n- Explicit numeric thresholds for frame-time/interaction/artifact/crash budgets: present\n- Mapping from thresholds to concrete harness/artifact outputs: present\n- Hardware-tier matrix and gate definitions (CI/nightly/go-no-go): present\n- Degradation/rollback trigger policy: present\n\nRemaining dependency-bound item is percentile refresh, not threshold definition. This should no longer block downstream consumers from using the SLO contract as authoritative baseline.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:49:53Z"},{"id":149,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.4","author":"RusticBear","text":"Updated SLO contract document to convert this bead from draft calibration notes into a frozen candidate contract with explicit dependency-bound refresh semantics.

Updated file:
- docs/resize-performance-slos.md

What changed:
- Status promoted to Candidate v1.0 (numeric SLO contract frozen for CI/nightly/go-no-go gating)
- Added explicit calibration intake section with wa-1u90p.1.3 artifact pointers
- Added current replay/build blocker taxonomy for fresh percentile extraction (E0308/E0277/E0609 + generate_scrollback replay mismatch)
- Recorded that prior scheduler const-trait blocker (E0658) is now resolved by wa-1u90p.2.3
- Tightened "Current Gaps" and "Dependency-Bound Sections" to distinguish enforceable thresholds now vs refresh-calibration after replay unblock

Acceptance check vs bead scope:
- Explicit numeric thresholds for frame-time/interaction/artifact/crash budgets: present
- Mapping from thresholds to concrete harness/artifact outputs: present
- Hardware-tier matrix and gate definitions (CI/nightly/go-no-go): present
- Degradation/rollback trigger policy: present

Remaining dependency-bound item is percentile refresh, not threshold definition. This should no longer block downstream consumers from using the SLO contract as authoritative baseline.
","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:50:03Z"}]}

Kept bead in_progress because final numeric calibration still depends on wa-1u90p.1.3 lock/memory profiles and consolidated percentile/artifact rollups.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:41:55Z"},{"id":143,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.5","author":"MaroonGlacier","text":"Follow-up integration: incorporated fresh wa-1u90p.1.3 handoff artifacts (docs/resize-lock-memory-profile-wa-1u90p.1.3.md + evidence bundle paths) into dossier, including explicit blocker taxonomy (generate_scrollback schema mismatch and compile blocker classes E0308/E0277/E0609/E0658). Ranking now references current profiling state and clearly marks confidence limits until percentile reruns are unblocked.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:43:39Z"},{"id":178,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.1.5","author":"VioletDune","text":"2026-02-14 VioletDune forced continuation claim (dependency-gated by wa-1u90p.1.3 status) and dossier update in docs/resize-baseline-bottleneck-dossier.md.  {"id":"wa-1u90p.2","title":"Resize Control Plane: Coalescing, Scheduling, and State Machine","description":"Implement an explicit resize control plane with latest-intent semantics, bounded work scheduling, and deterministic state transitions.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks for this track are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Track-level objectives are met with measurable evidence (correctness + performance where applicable).
- Validation artifacts and operational notes are captured for future maintainers.","design":"Explicit resize control plane","acceptance_criteria":"Coalescing, scheduler, and overload controls validated","notes":"No stale-intent backlog allowed","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.269560Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:29:43.440109Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","epic","reflow","resize","track-control-plane"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.269560Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:27.530331Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Track 2 Detailed Intent: Resize Control Plane

## Core requirement
Resize must be orchestrated through an explicit transaction model, not ad-hoc nested calls.

## Critical properties
- latest-intent wins
- stale-intent cancellation safety
- bounded queue growth
- frame-budget-aware scheduling
- introspectable lifecycle telemetry

## Failure handling
Overload/backpressure policy must preserve interaction even when quality work is deferred.

## Deliverable quality bar
Must be debuggable in production: operators need to see why work was deferred/cancelled/committed.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","title":"Author ADR for two-phase resize transactions and explicit state machine","description":"## Context
A robust resize architecture requires explicit transaction semantics and lifecycle invariants.

## Scope
Author the ADR for two-phase resize transactions, coalescing policy, cancellation behavior, and scheduler interaction contracts.

## Deliverables
- ADR with state diagram and transition guards
- Definition of latest-intent semantics and stale-work cancellation rules
- Failure-mode analysis (deadlock, starvation, queue overload) and mitigations
- Migration plan from current behavior to new control-plane behavior

## Why this matters
This bead is the architectural contract that prevents ad-hoc behavior drift across tracks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ADR includes complete state machine, invariants, and edge-case handling.
- Decision alternatives and tradeoffs are documented with rationale.
- At least one simulation/test fixture validates state transitions and cancellation semantics.
- Downstream tracks reference this ADR as normative behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletFinch","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.330095Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:58:15.136756Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:58:15.136743Z","close_reason":"Authored ADR-0011 + contract fixture tests; prework complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adr","reflow","resize","state-machine","track-control-plane"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:29.448076Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.330095Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","author":"VioletFinch","text":"Implemented blocked-safe prework slice for wa-1u90p.2.1:\n- Added ADR docs/adr/0011-resize-transaction-state-machine.md with two-phase transaction model, explicit state machine + guards, invariants, cancellation semantics, scheduler contract, failure-mode mitigations, alternatives, and migration plan.\n- Added contract fixture test crates/frankenterm-core/tests/resize_transaction_state_machine.rs covering happy path, superseded-boundary cancellation, storm coalescing (latest-intent wins), and stale-commit prevention.\n- Linked candidate 1 in docs/resize-baseline-bottleneck-dossier.md to ADR-0011.\nValidation:\n- cargo fmt --check: PASS\n- rustc --test crates/frankenterm-core/tests/resize_transaction_state_machine.rs && test run: PASS (4/4)\n- cargo check --all-targets / cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings: FAIL due unrelated pre-existing workspace errors in runtime.rs, workflows.rs, search/chunk_vector_store.rs and existing lint debt in chunking.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:57:15Z"}]}
